inclusions: Iron ores and concentrates (haematite and others, agglomerated and non-agglomerated); roasted iron pyrites
Manganese ores and concentrates, including ferruginous manganese ores with Mn content of 20% or more (dry weight)
Copper, nickel, cobalt, aluminium, lead, zinc, tin, chromium, tungsten ores and concentrates
Uranium and thorium ores and concentrates
Molybdenum ores and concentrates (roasted and other)
Titanium ores and concentrates (ilmenite and other)
Niobium, tantalum, vanadium or zirconium ores and concentrates
Precious metal ores and concentrates (silver, gold, platinum group)
Other ores and concentrates (antimony and other)
Granulated slag (slag sand) from the manufacture of iron or steel
Slag, dross (other than granulated slag), scalings and other waste from the manufacture of iron or steel
Slag, ash and residues (other than from iron/steel) containing metals, arsenic or their compounds — of a kind used in industry for extraction of metals or manufacture of their chemical compounds
Slag containing arsenic, mercury, thallium or their mixtures used for extraction or chemical manufacture
Other slag and ash including seaweed ash (kelp); ash and residues from incineration of municipal waste

exclusions.condition: Slag or similar industrial waste prepared as macadam (road-making material)
Natural magnesium carbonate (magnesite), whether or not calcined
Sludges from storage tanks of petroleum oils consisting mainly of such oils
Basic slag (from steel manufacturing, used as fertilizer)
Slag wool, rock wool or similar mineral wools
Waste or scrap of precious metal or metal clad with precious metal; other waste/scrap containing precious metal used principally for recovery of precious metal
Copper, nickel or cobalt mattes produced by any process of smelting

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Definition of 'ores' for headings 26.01 to 26.17
classification_rules.description: 'Ores' means minerals of mineralogical species ACTUALLY USED in the metallurgical industry for the extraction of: mercury, metals of heading 28.44 (radioactive), metals of Section XIV (precious metals) or Section XV (base metals). This applies EVEN IF intended for non-metallurgical purposes. HOWEVER, minerals submitted to processes NOT NORMAL to the metallurgical industry → NOT classified in 26.01–26.17.

classification_rules.rule: Unroasted vs. roasted iron pyrites — Ch.25 vs. Ch.26 boundary
classification_rules.description: Unroasted iron pyrites (FeS2) → Heading 25.02 (Chapter 25). Roasted iron pyrites (calcined/oxidised) → Heading 26.01.20 (Chapter 26). The roasting process moves the product from Chapter 25 to Chapter 26. This is the key pyrites boundary.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Heading 26.20 scope (non-iron/steel slag with metals)
classification_rules.description: Heading 26.20 applies ONLY to: (a) slag, ash and residues used in industry for EXTRACTION OF METALS or as basis for MANUFACTURE OF CHEMICAL COMPOUNDS OF METALS — excluding ash/residues from incineration of municipal waste (→ 26.21); and (b) slag, ash and residues containing ARSENIC (whether or not containing metals), of a kind used for extraction of arsenic/metals or manufacture of their chemical compounds.

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 2 — Arsenic/mercury/thallium slag priority in 2620.60
classification_rules.description: Slag, ash and residues containing arsenic, mercury, thallium or their MIXTURES, of a kind used for extraction of arsenic or those metals or for manufacture of their chemical compounds → MUST be classified in subheading 2620.60 regardless of which metal is predominant. This is a mandatory priority rule within heading 26.20.

classification_rules.rule: Iron ore classification by agglomeration and type
classification_rules.description: Heading 26.01 sub-classifies iron ores by: (1) Non-agglomerated vs. Agglomerated (sintered/pelletised); (2) Within each: haematite (SEN) vs. other. Roasted iron pyrites separately → 2601.20. Haematite (Fe2O3) get special SEN status under Vietnam tariff.

classification_rules.rule: Granulated slag vs. other iron/steel slag — 26.18 vs. 26.19
classification_rules.description: From iron or steel manufacture: GRANULATED slag (water-quenched, glassy particles, used for cement/blastfurnace) → 26.18. Other slag, dross (non-granulated), scalings and other waste → 26.19. Both are from iron/steel manufacture; the granulation process determines the heading.
